revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Danjōzan Kofun]][[ja:壇場山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Himeji, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Danjōzuka Kofun | native_name =壇場山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Danjyo-zan Kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Danjōzuka Kofun, posterior circular portion to the left | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Danjōzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.822433|N|134.735436|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|20.76|N|134|44|7.57|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Danjōzuka Kofun'''|壇場山古墳|||qq=}} refers to a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Mikuninochō-Kokubunji neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Four tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163241|title=壇場山古墳, 第一、二、三古墳|trans-title=Danjozan kofun, daiichi、ni、sankofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Danjōzuka Kofun== The Danjōzuka Kofun is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=Fluvial terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} on the left bank of the Ichikawa River in eastern Himeji City. Per local tradition, it was constructed by {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, who used it as a ceremonial platform. The tumulus has been surveyed, but no [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the northwest and has an overall length of around 143 meters, making it the third largest in the region. It is constructed in three layers, and has almost identical dimensions to the Nakatsuyama Kofun in [[:en:Fujiidera, Osaka|Fujiidera, Osaka]], with artifacts on both the east and west sides of the central constriction, indicating a strong connection with the {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|qid=Q633881|lt=Yamato Kingdom|1=Yamato period}}. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat with a width of about 10 to 20 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The lid stone of a stone sarcophagus is partially exposed on the top of the posterior circular portion. This stone measures 2.7 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, and there are a total of eight rope-hanging protrusions, two on each side. However, as it is resting on the top of the mound, it is not certain whether or not there is a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] underneath, and it is also unclear if the sarcophagus has been opened. An article dated November 23, 1903 in the Kobe Asahi Shimbun debated whether or not it should be opened, and another article dated from 1910 mentioned that some 200 iron artifacts including ten iron swords were excavated in the northwestern part of the tumulus, but it is not clear whether or not this is a different location from this sarcophagus. Since the existing sarcophagus is not located in the center of the posterior circular portion, and there have been many instances of multiple burial facilities in other ''kofun'', this remains a possibility. The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the first half of the 5th century in the middleof the Kofun period, and to be the grave of the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima|1=Harima Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ;Overall length: 142.8 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 83 meter diameter x 13.2 meters high x 3-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 87 meters wide x 11.4 meters high x 3-tier The tumulus was once surrounded by ten smaller ''kofun'', of which eight have been destroyed due to residential housing development. The remaining two are part of the National Historic Site designation. The tumuli are about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Gochaku Station|en|Gochaku Station|qid=Q1042454|lt=Gochaku Station|1=Gochaku Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'yō Main Line|en|San'yō Main Line|qid=Q1193436|lt=San'yō Main Line|1=San'yō Main Line}}. The {{nihongo|'''Rindō Higashizuka Kofun'''|林堂東塚古墳|||qq=}} is listed as "No.1 Kofun" in the National Historic Site designation. It is a circular {{nihongo| ''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|||qq=}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 18 meter, located on the east side of the posterior circular portion of the Danjōzuka Kofun. It is in very poor preservation with much its mound shaved away. The {{nihongo|'''Kushinodō Kofun'''|櫛之堂古墳|||qq=}} is listed as "No.2 Kofun" in the National Historic Site designation. It is also an ''enpun'' and is located on the southwest side of the posterior circular portion of the Danjōzuka Kofun. It is also in very poor preservation with much its mound shaved away.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==Yamanokoshi Kofun== The {{nihongo| Yamanokoshi Kofun|山之越古墳|||qq=}} is listed as "No.3 Kofun" in the National Historic Site designation. It is located about 150 meters northwest of the main axis of the Danjōyama tumulus. The tumulus is a square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 60 meters on each side, with a height of seven meters. This makes it the largest ''hōfun'' in the prefecture, but the northern half of the tumulus has been destroyed, and it is in poor preservation. Some traces of ''fukiishi'' and small fragments of ''haniwa'' gave been found, and they mound was once surrounded by a moat with a width of 17 meters. A stone sarcophagus is currently exposed at the top of the mound, and as no trace of a stone-lined burlap chamber have been found it is believed to have been directly buried in the tumulus. The length of the sarcophagus is shorter than that of the Danjōzuka Kofun, and it is considered to be of later date from its style. When the sarcophagus was opened in 1897, the bones indie were found to be orientated to the north and resting on a bad of white river stones. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a 16.4-cm {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and an iron sword. These artifacts were not preserved properly and the mirror and sword have severely deteriorated, whereas the other artifacts, including additional bronze mirrors and iron swords found outside the sarcophagus, have been scattered and their whereabouts are currently unknown. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middleof the 5th century, following the Danjōzuka Kofun, and that it is of the less-prestigious ''hōfun''-style is an indication that the local rulers ad by that time become more subservient to Yamato authority.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==Gallery== <gallery> 801029 Danjozan Kofun.jpg|Danjōzuka Kofun in 1980 Danjyo-zan Kofun sekkan.JPG|Exposed stone sarcophagus of the Danjōzuka Kofun Danjyo-zan Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|CRindō Higashizuka Kofun Danjyo-zan Kofun baizuka-2-2.jpg|Kushinodō Kofun Yamanokoshi Kofun funkyu-1.jpg|Yamanokoshi Kofun </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|qid=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links==
[[en:Danjōzan Kofun]]
